title: Police investigating after dead body found near home owned by Pressley’s husband
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News Few details have been released about what exactly happened near the home Saturday, but police are still investigating.
A body was found Saturday near a property owned by Congresswoman Ayanna Pressley's husband.

Lane Turner/The Boston Globe By Darin Zullo July 19, 2026 | 11:01 AM 1 minute to read Boston police are investigating after a dead body was found Saturday near a Mattapan home owned by Congresswoman Ayanna Pressley’s husband.

title: Healey aims to close great divide of resale ticket prices with new proposal
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News Dubbed “The Great Divide Act,” the legislation would cap resale prices, limit fees, and prohibit deceptive practices.
People line up outside before a Noah Kahan concert outside Fenway Park on July 11.
Christian Kantosky/The Boston Globe By Darin Zullo July 19, 2026 | 10:23 AM 2 minutes to read Gov.

title: Rhode Island couple unexpectedly become parents while on vacation in Florida
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News The couple was preparing to set sail on a cruise when they got the surprise of a lifetime: they were going to be parents.
By Darin Zullo July 19, 2026 | 9:14 AM 1 minute to read While on vacation in Florida, a Rhode Island couple picked up an unexpected souvenir — a new family member.
Lauren Siwinski and Gary Glenn had just arrived in the Tampa Bay area to set sail on a cruise when Siwinski began experiencing severe abdominal pain, HCA Florida Brandon Hospital said on Facebook.

title: Juvenile humpback whale washes ashore in Winthrop
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Local News An investigation is underway into the death of the young whale, which was discovered lodged under a private dock on Wednesday.
Animal stranding response crews inspect a 35,000-pound humpback whale that washed up stuck under a private dock in Winthrop on Wednesday.
Whale & Dolphin Conservation USA A juvenile humpback whale washed ashore and became lodged beneath a dock in Winthrop on Wednesday, prompting an investigation by marine rescue officials.

title: Jahmai Jones hits memorable first home run for Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox In front of the Fenway Crowd, Jones launched a two-run homer over the Green Monster Saturday.
Boston Red Sox's Jahmai Jones celebrates after his two-run home run in the second inning of a baseball game against the Tampa Bay Rays, Saturday, July 18, 2026, in Boston.
(AP Photo/Charles Krupa) Jahmai Jones hasn’t been in Boston very long, but he’s making quite the first impression.

title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox Abreu is the first Red Sox player to accomplish that feat since Mookie Betts in 2016.

BOSTON, MASSACHUSETTS - JULY 18: Wilyer Abreu #52 of the Boston Red Sox celebrates after hitting the go-ahead home run in the bottom of the seventh inning against the Tampa Bay Rays at Fenway Park on July 18, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
(Photo by Joe Buglewicz/Getty Images) Wilyer Abreu has been on another level at Fenway Park this weekend.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox Boston trailed by three runs entering the seventh inning.
Naturally, it won.
The Red Sox found a way to top their doubleheader sweep from Friday with Saturday’s jaw-dropping victory.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Celtics James's highly anticipated free agency decision is looming.
LeBron James seems set to make his free agency decision soon.

(Photo by Roy Rochlin/Getty Images for Fanatics) LeBron James’s free agency decision is the biggest storyline left in the NBA offseason, and it’s reportedly even forcing the NBA to hold off on releasing its 2026-27 schedule.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: New England Patriots Brady went viral after smacking Logan Paul’s face on Friday.
(L-R) Chris Costa, Wesley Sneijder, Tom Brady, Karl-Anthony Towns, and Logan Paul speak onstage during the This is Why We Collect panel at Fanatics Fest NYC 2026 on July 17, 2026 in New York City.
John Nacion/Getty Images for Fanatics By Kaley Brown July 18, 2026 | 5:40 PM 2 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Local News A 28-year-old man was allegedly beaten and dragged to a nearby residence, where police say he was tied up with an extension cord and left injured and bleeding.
Four Nantucket men are facing kidnapping and multiple assault charges after authorities say they attacked a man near a playground on Thursday afternoon.
According to a police report obtained by The Boston Globe, the suspects allegedly beat the 28-year-old victim and tied him up with an extension cord on Essex Road near Winter Park.
